mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, causing capital inflows into equities and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns following month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS are negative in more than 50% of non-overlapping instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6.0% triggers recessionary earnings downgrade expectations, increasing equity risk premia and causing negative S&P 500 returns as portfolio managers reduce cyclical exposure.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 55% of triggered months during any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and industrial production signals a broad demand-supply expansion that boosts corporate revenue visibility, driving positive S&P 500 returns; either variable alone fails to capture the breadth of the expansion.
failure_condition: The joint z-score interaction term of RSAFS and INDPRO fails to produce a Spearman rank correlation above 0.05 with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month increases in CPI accelerate expectations of further Fed hikes, compressing equity valuations through higher real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, month-over-month CPI increases coincide with positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in industrial production signals reduced manufacturing profitability and economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of non-overlapping periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below one million units signals a structural demand collapse in residential investment, dragging down construction-sensitive equities and aggregate S&P 500 earnings expectations.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 1000, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in greater than 55% of triggered months during any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth signals expanding household income and consumption capacity, lifting corporate sales expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of non-overlapping instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with rising CPI creates stagflationary pressure that squeezes margins and de-rates equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of UNRATE and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with a t-statistic below -1.5 over any 3-year rolling window.
